participants.professional_persona: 공과대학 석사 과정에 재학 중이며, 인공지능 연구실에서 학술 활동을 병행하고 있습니다. BK21 교육연구단의 지원을 받아 연구에 전념하고 있으며, 온라인 미팅을 통해 다른 참여자들과 연구 애로사항을 공유하고 있습니다.
participants.skills_and_expertise: 딥러닝 모델 설계 및 구현에 능숙하며, 파이썬과 텐서플로우를 활용한 연구 경험이 풍부합니다.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: 향후 국내 대학원에서 박사 학위를 취득한 후, 글로벌 IT 기업의 연구소에서 인공지능 연구원으로 일하는 것이 목표입니다.

participants.name: KAIST에서
participants.professional_persona: KAIST에서 전기및전자공학 박사학위를 취득하고, 대전 지역 BK21 교육연구팀에서 박사후연구원으로 재직 중입니다. 차세대 반도체 소자용 강유전체 박막 공정 연구를 수행하고 있습니다.
participants.skills_and_expertise: 원자층 증착 장비 운용과 전기적 특성 분석에 정통하며, 반도체 공정 장비 업체와의 공동 연구 경험이 풍부합니다.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: 국내 대기업 반도체 연구소로 이직하여 양산 기술 개발에 직접 참여하고, 궁극적으로는 차세대 메모리 소자 상용화를 이끄는 전문가가 되는 것이 목표입니다.

participants.name: 전남
participants.professional_persona: 전남 지역 거점 국립대학에서 농업생명공학 교육연구단 행정을 전담하는 전문직원입니다. 연구단의 예산 집행과 정산, 자체평가 보고서 제출을 지원하며 연구단이 원활히 운영되도록 돕습니다.
participants.skills_and_expertise: 국가연구개발사업 예산 관리 및 회계, 연구 성과 데이터 입력 및 관리, 연구단 관련 규정 해석에 밝습니다.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: 교육연구단이 컨설팅을 희망할 경우 신속히 서류를 준비하여 연구단의 발전에 기여하고, 행정 전문가로서 BK21 사업 전반의 노하우를 쌓고 싶습니다.

participants.name: 연세대학교에서 (ij8wo4)
participants.professional_persona: 연세대학교에서 경제학 박사 과정을 하고 있으며, BK21 미래인재양성사업의 지원을 받고 있습니다. 박사 과정생으로서 매월 130만 원의 장학금과 함께 연구 집중을 위한 환경을 제공받고 있습니다. 한국은행이나 KDI 같은 경제정책 연구기관에 진출하는 것을 희망합니다.
participants.skills_and_expertise: 계량경제학 모델링과 빅데이터 경제 분석에 강점이 있으며, Stata와 R을 능숙하게 활용합니다.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: 한국 경제의 불평등과 고용 구조에 대한 깊이 있는 연구를 바탕으로 정책 제안을 할 수 있는 연구자가 되는 것이 목표입니다.

participants.name: 경북대학교에서
participants.professional_persona: 경북대학교에서 문화인류학 박사학위를 받고, 대구·경북 지역 BK21 교육연구팀에서 박사후연구원으로 활동 중입니다. 재일코리안의 정체성 변화와 초국가적 네트워크에 관한 민족지적 연구를 진행하고 있습니다.
participants.skills_and_expertise: 장기 현지조사와 생애사 인터뷰 방법론에 능하며, 일본어 구사 능력을 바탕으로 일본 내 아카이브 자료 분석에도 강점이 있습니다.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: 한국과 일본을 오가며 디아스포라 연구의 권위자로 성장하고, 향후 대학 부설 연구소의 책임연구원이 되는 것을 꿈꿉니다.

initial_posts.narrative: As a postdoctoral researcher at a BK21 education research group in Daegu, I see the total funding allocated to Hanyang University's BK21 program for 2022 as the national total of 408,080 million KRW, since Hanyang is one of the 68 universities. The graduate scholarship slots are estimated based on the national average: about 12,600 master's and 6,400 doctoral/doctoral candidate students per year across all BK21 groups, totaling ~19,000 slots. For postdoc positions, with 577 education research groups and each group typically supporting 2-3 postdocs, I estimate around 1,200 positions nationally, though Hanyang's share would be proportional to its number of groups.
evidence: The Ministry of Education's 2022 budget for BK21 education research group support is 408,080 million KRW, as stated in the policy context.
Graduate student participation is about 12,600 per year in future talent training and 6,400 in innovation talent training, summing to ~19,000 slots nationally.
With 577 education research groups and typical postdoc support of 2-3 per group, national postdoc positions are around 1,200-1,700; I use 1,200 as a conservative estimate.

initial_posts.narrative: As an administrative staff member at a regional national university's agriculture and biotechnology education research group, I see the funds disbursed according to the policy's two-installment schedule: 70% in March (285,656 million KRW) and 30% in September (122,424 million KRW), totaling the full allocated budget of 408,080 million KRW. The evaluation panels are convened for three formal meetings: the annual evaluation, the mid-term evaluation, and the compliance check panel. Consulting sessions are provided to the 280 education research groups that requested consulting, as stated in the policy context. Compliance checks are executed on the 205 innovative talent training groups, which are subject to implementation checks. Budget adjustments and reallocations are handled based on evaluation outcomes, but at this phase, the initial disbursement follows the schedule without adjustments.

In my experience, alumni progress slowly, and the impact on national rankings is incremental.

initial_posts.narrative: As a master's student in the AI lab at Hanyang University's College of Engineering, I see the impact phase as the culmination of a decade of sustained investment in graduate talent development. The BK21 program's inputs (408,080 million KRW, 19,000 graduate slots, 1,200 postdoc positions) and activities (full disbursement, evaluations, consulting, compliance checks) have produced substantial outputs: 57,000 publications and 11,400 international conference presentations. These outputs, combined with the program's emphasis on international collaboration (20% rate) and high full-time faculty lecture ratio (90%), have gradually improved national innovation capacity. Over a decade, the sustained investment has enhanced the quality of graduate training, leading to more high-impact research. The dissemination of outstanding group achievements through webzines and forums has spread best practices across institutions, raising the overall S&T ecosystem's productivity. I estimate South Korea's rank in global S&T talent competitiveness improves to 14, reflecting the program's impact on STEM fields, especially engineering and AI. For high-impact publications per 1000 researchers, I estimate 4.2, based on my lab's experience where about 5% of our publications are in top 1% cited journals, and the program's focus on quality research. This is higher than the estimates from Kyungpook (3.5) and KAIST (2.8) because my perspective from a top engineering lab at Hanyang shows higher productivity in high-impact work, especially in AI where top conference papers are highly cited. The Yonsei economics student's estimate of 1.2 reflects their field's lower publication rates, but for engineering, the impact is stronger.
evidence: The BK21 program's sustained investment over a decade has improved graduate training quality, leading to more high-impact research; my lab's experience shows that about 5% of our publications are in top 1% cited journals, and with 57,000 publications from the program, this yields approximately 2,850 high-impact publications, or 4.2 per 1000 researchers (19,000 graduate students + 1,200 postdocs = 20,200 researchers).

The program's emphasis on international collaboration (20% rate) and high full-time faculty lecture ratio (90%) enhances research quality and network effects, which are key drivers of high-impact publications and national competitiveness.

Dissemination of outstanding group achievements through webzines and forums spreads best practices across institutions, raising the overall S&T ecosystem's productivity and improving South Korea's global rank in talent competitiveness.

My estimate of rank 14 is consistent with the program's focus on STEM fields, especially engineering and AI, where South Korea has strong global standing; the Kyungpook and KAIST estimates (15 and 16) are reasonable but slightly conservative given the program's impact on top-tier output.

The national S&T talent competitiveness rank improves slowly because the BK21 program's benefits are concentrated in STEM fields like engineering, while agriculture and social sciences have less impact on global rankings.
The dissemination of outstanding group achievements through webzines and forums helps spread best practices, but systemic changes in research culture take time and are uneven across disciplines.

Other participants' estimates vary widely (rank 14-18, high-impact publications 1.2-4.2), but my administrative experience with data entry across multiple groups supports a moderate estimate for agriculture and biotechnology.
